1.description: Prevalence of odd-lot trades in study stocks The figure presents a time-series plot of the fraction of trades that are odd for the study stocks. Our total sample of 1,736 stocks is divided into five groups based on the average VWAP observed across full trading days in December 2020.The stock groups are defined by stock price with cut points of $20, $50, $100, and $250.

2.description: TAQ order book at NBBO versus May Street order book at NBBO Panel A shows the NBBO for AMZN at 13:25:00 on March 31, 2021, observed using TAQ Data. Panel B shows the NBBO and all better-priced odd-lot quotes for AMZN at 13:25:00 on March 31, 2021, observed using LSEG Data.

3.description: Distribution of order sizes by price group (Q1 2021) Data represent all messages to add liquidity across 16 stock exchanges for all sample stocks between January 4, 2021, and March 3, 2021, for order sizes fewer than 500 shares.

4.description: Illustration of odd-lot runs on the offer and bid for Restoration Hardware The figure plots the evolution of the top of the book for odd-lot orders as well as the NBB and NBO between 10:51:43.5307 and 10:51:46 on January 4, 2021.

5.description: Receiver operating characteristic curves of SIP model versus OL model Area under the Receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve showing the predictive performance of the SIP model versus the OL model using the test data for two SIP models and two OL models. Panel A presents results for the SIP model using group 1 stocks. Panel B presents results for the OL model using group 1 stocks. Panel C presents results for the SIP model using group 5 stocks. Panel D presents results for the OL model using group 5 stocks.

6.description: SHAP values for Chipotle OL model prediction Illustration of the SHAP values for all features in the OL model for the model’s prediction for Chipotle at the beginning of 09:34:00 on January 8, 2021.

7.description: Average SHAP values for features in the group 5 training data The figure summarizes the mean of the absolute value of all SHAP values calculated across 1.7 million observations in the training data for group 5 stocks.

8.description: Distribution of SHAP values for all features of group 5 training data: OL model The plot depicts the relative importance, impact, and contribution of different features in the group 5 training data for the OL model. Positive SHAP values indicate a positive classification. The summary plot combines feature importance with feature effects. The features on the y-axis are ordered according to their importance (top=high importance). Each point on the summary plot is a SHAP value for a feature and an instance (i.e., a single stock-day-minute) in the training data set. The position of each point on the x-axis shows the impact that the feature has on the classification model’s prediction for a given instance. The color represents the high (red) to low (blue) values of the feature.

9.description: Daily returns for group 1 versus group 5 stocks Average daily returns from a trading strategy that uses the SIP model and OL model to execute a round-trip trade that begins with a purchase at the commencement of a stock-minute and ends with a sale at VWAPt+1⁠. Panel A presents returns for group 1 stocks. Panel B presents returns for group 5 stocks.

10.description: Distribution of daily returns with a 70% probability threshold: SIP model versus OL model The distribution of daily returns across the 61 trading days for the SIP model compared to the OL model when the models use a probability threshold of 70% to predict a positive label.
